evidence_index.text: The Claimant was admitted to hospital on an emergency basis on 15 July 2014 with what transpired to be an Addison's crisis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She was in the intensive care unit for some 3 weeks. She needed a left ventricular assist device.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She then subsequently suffered a stroke in February 2015.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: One of several visits that she had made to her GP's surgery had been on Friday 4 July 2014 when she was seen by the Defendant.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The upshot of that visit was this: certain drugs were prescribed; repeat tests were arranged to take place the following Monday; she was advised by the Defendant that if her condition worsened she should call the out of hours telephone line or attend hospital A&E.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The proposed settlement subject to that approval involved the payment of £700,000, without any admission of liability, in full and final settlement.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: I am satisfied that this settlement is in the Claimant's best interests.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: I approve this settlement in the exercise of my inherent jurisdiction.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Defendant's case is that there was no negligence and no breach of any duty, that the response at the appointment on 4 July 2014 was a proper discharge of the clinical duties.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Claimant's case was that there was a negligent breach of duty by the Defendant on 4 July 2014.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with symptoms related to Addison's disease.

requestables.name: Cortisol Level
requestables.description: Serum cortisol level to assess adrenal function

reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt urgent treatment if low

timeline_phases.name: Initial GP Visit
timeline_phases.description: Patient visits GP with symptoms suggestive of Addison's disease

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Crisis
timeline_phases.description: Patient's condition worsens leading to emergency admission

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presented with symptoms suggestive of Addison's disease
decision_points.prompt: What should have been the immediate management step?

options.description: Urgently admit to hospital for further evaluation and management
options.clinical_reasoning: Urgent admission would allow for immediate treatment and monitoring

options.description: Prescribe medication and arrange follow-up tests
options.clinical_reasoning: Delayed appropriate care leading to crisis

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant prescribed medication and arranged follow-up tests
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed outpatient management was sufficient

expected_action_court.description: Urgent hospital admission should have been arranged
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence suggested immediate care was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to admit led to Addison's crisis
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Breach of duty in failing to provide urgent care

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed Addison's crisis requiring intensive care

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to admit patient urgently
Causation established - earlier intervention would have prevented crisis

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Urgent admission would have prevented Addison's crisis

court_findings.finding: Settlement approved without admission of liability
court_findings.legal_significance: Settlement in best interest of Claimant
